checklist_item.title: Falsify Invariant on ACTION-VERIFY-SIGNATURE: Signature verification must be deterministic: for identical inputs (h,

reachability.notes: Different math libraries or constant-time vs non-constant-time implementations could yield different results for edge-case inputs.

checklist_item.detection_procedure:
1. Invariant: Signature verification must be deterministic: for identical inputs (h, r, s, qx, qy), all implementations must produce identical output (either 32-byte success or empty).
2. Enumerate all state transitions affecting 'ACTION-VERIFY-SIGNATURE'.
3. Design test sequences to violate the invariant through:
   a. Rapid successive state transitions
   b. Boundary value inputs to state-changing operations
   c. Concurrent or re-entrant state modifications
4. Verify the invariant holds after each transition.
5. Check edge cases: empty state, maximum state, overflow.
6. Severity justification: Non-deterministic verification causes consensus split across all clients processing the same block.
executable_checks: Create state transition sequence targeting ACTION-VERIFY-SIGNATURE invariant violation
Test boundary values for state transitions affecting ACTION-VERIFY-SIGNATURE
Verify invariant holds under concurrent modifications
checklist_item.notes: Source property: PROP-W0-EIP7951-INV-003. Type: Invariant. Primary element: ACTION-VERIFY-SIGNATURE.

checklist_item.title: Verify post-boundary validation at STATE-COLUMN-SIDECAR-RECEIVED supporting EDGE-CHECK-FORMAT

reachability.notes: Attacker sends sidecar with wrong cell count, potentially causing buffer overflows or incorrect KZG verification.

checklist_item.detection_procedure:
1. Trace data flow from boundary EDGE-CHECK-FORMAT through node STATE-COLUMN-SIDECAR-RECEIVED.
2. Property context: A DataColumnSidecar received from the P2P network must have its cell count match the expected number of cells per column before acceptance.
3. Verify that STATE-COLUMN-SIDECAR-RECEIVED correctly validates/processes data that has crossed the trust boundary.
4. Check internal invariants are maintained even if boundary validation is partially bypassed.
5. Test with edge-case inputs that pass boundary validation but stress internal handling.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for ACTION-VERIFY-KZG-PROOFS: Before accepting a data_column_sidecar, the node MUST verify verify_data_column_

reachability.notes: ASSUM-FULU-P2P-03: binding property prevents forgery without original blob

checklist_item.detection_procedure:
1. Identify the trust boundary at ACTION-VERIFY-KZG-PROOFS (trust level: UNTRUSTED, target: unknown).
2. Property: Before accepting a data_column_sidecar, the node MUST verify verify_data_column_sidecar_kzg_proofs: batch cell KZG proof verification of column data against commitments (rule 11). Invalid KZG proofs MUST result in REJECT with peer penalty per ASSUM-FULU-P2P-03.
3. Target interface: N/A.
4. Attack vectors: generic boundary fuzzing.
5. Craft adversarial inputs crossing this boundary to test validation completeness.
6. Verify all input validation, authentication, and data sanitization are enforced.
7. Check for bypass paths that skip validation.
8. Severity justification: KZG proof verification is the final cryptographic guarantee of data integrity. Bypass would allow propagation of arbitrary data, breaking data availability and potentially causing consensus failures a

checklist_item.title: Falsify Post-condition on ACTION-VALIDATE-BLOB-COUNT: After blob commitment validation, a block is accepted only if len(body

reachability.notes: An attacker-proposed block with excessive blob commitments must be rejected. Failure to reject could cause state bloat.

checklist_item.detection_procedure:
1. Post-condition: After blob commitment validation, a block is accepted only if len(body.blob_kzg_commitments) <= resolved max_blobs_per_block. Blocks exceeding the limit must be rejected.
2. Execute the operation with various valid inputs.
3. After each execution, verify:
   a. All expected side-effects occurred correctly
   b. No unexpected state changes occurred
   c. Return values match the post-condition specification
4. Test with boundary inputs that are valid but extreme.
5. Check partial execution/failure scenarios.
6. Severity justification: Accepting blocks with excessive blobs would cause state bloat and consensus disagreement with correct nodes.

Test partial failure scenarios for post-condition violations

2. Property context: The parent block header must contain valid excess_blob_gas and blob_gas_used fields before excess blob gas calculation. Both fields must be non-negative and blob_gas_used must not exceed max blob gas 
3. Verify that STATE-PARENT-HEADER-READ correctly validates/processes data that has crossed the trust boundary.

reachability.notes: If verified column count can decrease (e.g., due to re-verification bugs), the node could oscillate between available/unavailable states.

2. Enumerate all state transitions affecting 'ACTION-VERIFY-COLUMN-PROOFS'.
3. Design test sequences to violate the invariant through:
   a. Rapid successive state transitions

5. Check edge cases: empty state, maximum state, overflow.
6. Severity justification: Incorrect column counting could cause individual nodes to make wrong DA decisions but unlikely to affect >33% of network.

reachability.notes: Missing or corrupted field during migration causes state root mismatch.

checklist_item.detection_procedure:
1. Post-condition: After field migration, every Electra state field MUST be present in the Fulu state object with identical values, preserving validator continuity and historical integrity.
2. Execute the operation with various valid inputs.

checklist_item.title: Falsify Pre-condition on ACTION-VALIDATE-RECOVERY-INPUTS: Cell indices provided for recovery MUST be in strictly ascending order

reachability.notes: Duplicate or unordered indices from P2P could cause vanishing polynomial construction to fail or produce incorrect results.

checklist_item.detection_procedure:
1. Pre-condition: Cell indices provided for recovery MUST be in strictly ascending order with no duplicates.
2. Enumerate inputs that should be rejected by this pre-condition.
3. Attempt bypass with:
   a. Invalid/malformed inputs that may slip through validation
   b. Edge case values (zero, max, negative, empty)
   c. Type confusion or encoding tricks
4. Verify correct rejection of all invalid inputs.
5. Check if bypassing leads to exploitable state.
6. Severity justification: Incorrect recovery due to duplicate indices could cause inconsistent data availability on >5% of nodes.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for secp256r1 Public Key Coordinate Bounds Validation

reachability.notes: Attacker controls qx and qy values in calldata.

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Craft a transaction calling the secp256r1 precompile (address 0x100) with public key coordinates qx=p (field modulus), qy=p, qx=2^256-1, qy=2^256-1, and qx=p-1, qy=p-1. 2. Execute the transaction across multiple client implementations. 3. Verify all clients return empty output (0 bytes) for qx >= p or qy >= p. 4. Verify qx=p-1 and qy=p-1 are accepted (if on curve). 5. Check that no client panics, returns different-length output, or produces different success/failure results.
executable_checks: Deploy test contract that calls precompile with boundary coordinates
Compare return values across geth, nethermind, besu, erigon, reth
Fuzz qx and qy in range [p-10, p+10] and [2^256-10, 2^256-1]
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W0-EIP7951-PRECOND-003. Boundary edge EDGE-TX-TO-PRECOMPILE. Trust level: UNTRUSTED. Covers nodes: ACTION-CHECK-PUBKEY-BOUNDS, STATE-PUBKEY-BOUNDS-VALID, STATE-PUBKEY-BOUNDS-INVALID. Different big-integer libraries may handle values >= p differently, causing consensus splits.

checklist_item.title: Verify Post-KZG-Validation State Transitions for DataColumnSidecar

reachability.notes: After KZG validation passes, sidecar must be correctly stored and made available for sampling.

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Send a valid DataColumnSidecar with correct KZG proofs. 2. Verify the node transitions to STATE-SIDECAR-VALIDATED. 3. Verify the sidecar is stored in the column store. 4. Verify the sidecar is available for sampling requests. 5. Verify the sidecar can be served to other peers via Req/Resp.
executable_checks: Send valid sidecar and verify storage
Query stored sidecar via Req/Resp and verify data integrity
Verify sidecar counts toward data availability quorum
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W1-DASCORE-PRECOND-004. Supporting node check for boundary property. Validates correct state transition after KZG verification passes.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for DataColumnSidecar Slot Timeliness Check

reachability.notes: Attacker can send sidecars with future or stale slots.

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Send a DataColumnSidecar with a slot far in the future (current_slot + 100). 2. Verify it is rejected (IGNORE). 3. Send a sidecar with slot = current_slot + 1 (within MAXIMUM_GOSSIP_CLOCK_DISPARITY). 4. Verify handling is correct. 5. Send a sidecar with slot below finalization. 6. Verify it is rejected. 7. Test at exact MAXIMUM_GOSSIP_CLOCK_DISPARITY boundary.
executable_checks: Send sidecar with future slot beyond clock disparity
Send sidecar at exact disparity boundary
Send sidecar below finalized slot
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W2-FULU-GOSSIP-PRECOND-03. Boundary property, Boundary Guard mindset. Step 3 of the 13-step gossip validation pipeline.

checklist_item.title: Falsify Invariant: Proposer Lookahead Initialized Before Queries at Fork Activation

reachability.notes: Querying proposer before lookahead initialization would return undefined/wrong results.

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Set up a node at exactly the Fulu fork activation slot. 2. Immediately query get_beacon_proposer_index before the state transition completes. 3. Verify the lookahead is initialized as part of the state transition, not lazily. 4. Test concurrent access patterns: block processing and proposer queries happening simultaneously. 5. Verify no default/zero values are returned for uninitialized lookahead.
executable_checks: Query proposer index at exact fork activation slot
Verify initialization ordering in state transition
Test for race conditions in concurrent access
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3-FULU-BEACON-LOOKAHEAD-INV-002. Internal property (Invariant), Formal Verification mindset. CRITICAL: uninitialized lookahead at fork activation is a consensus split vector.

checklist_item.title: Falsify Post-condition: No Attestation When Data Unavailable

reachability.notes: If node attests despite data unavailability, it supports potentially invalid blocks.

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Set up a node where data availability sampling fails (not enough columns retrieved). 2. Verify the node does NOT produce an attestation for the block. 3. Test with partial availability: some columns valid, some missing, below threshold. 4. Verify the node retries sampling before giving up. 5. After DA failure, verify the node does not change its mind if columns arrive late.
executable_checks: Simulate DA sampling failure and verify no attestation
Test with partial column availability below threshold
Verify no late attestation after DA failure declared
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W5-EIP7594-DAS-POSTCOND-002. Internal property (Post-condition), Formal Verification mindset. False DA attestations are a critical safety violation.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for Block Gas Limit Enforcement

reachability.notes: Blocks where cumulative gas exceeds block gas limit must be rejected.

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Construct a block where total gas_used exceeds the gas_limit in the header. 2. Propagate via P2P. 3. Verify all EL clients reject the block. 4. Test at exactly gas_limit (accepted) and gas_limit+1 (rejected). 5. Verify the default 60M gas limit is correctly applied post-Fusaka. 6. Test with gas limit voting mechanism to ensure convergence toward 60M.
executable_checks: Craft block with gas_used > gas_limit
Cross-client rejection verification
Verify 60M default gas limit post-Fusaka
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W7-EIP7935-GASECO-INV-001. Boundary edge EDGE-ADVERSARIAL-BLOCK-P2P. Trust level: UNTRUSTED. EIP-7935 sets default gas limit to 60M.

checklist_item.title: Verify side-effects of justified checkpoint update during on_block processing
checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Initialize store with baseline justified checkpoint. 2. Construct block at epoch boundary with various justified checkpoint configurations. 3. Process block via on_block and capture store's justified checkpoint state. 4. Verify expected behavior: if block's epoch boundary state has higher justified checkpoint, store updates; otherwise store unchanged. 5. Test for unexpected side-effects: finalized checkpoint modifications, other store state changes, partial updates. 6. Test edge cases: blocks not at epoch boundary, equal justified checkpoints, malformed checkpoint data. 7. Document deviations from post-condition.
executable_checks: Process epoch boundary block with higher justified checkpoint - verify store.justified updates
Process epoch boundary block with lower justified checkpoint - verify store.justified unchanged
Process epoch boundary block with equal justified checkpoint - verify store.justified unchanged
Process non-epoch-boundary block - verify justified checkpoint unchanged regardless of block state
Verify finalized checkpoint is not modified during justified update
Test block with malformed checkpoint data - verify graceful handling
Verify no other store components are affected by justified checkpoint update
Test sequential epoch boundary blocks - verify monotonic justified checkpoint progression
checklist_item.notes: Traced from PROP-W0-FULUFC-POSTCOND-004 covering on_block justified checkpoint update logic

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for EDGE-PARSE-0x13: Cells (0x13) response format validation
checklist_item.risk_category: Remote Code Execution / DoS / Memory Corruption
checklist_item.detection_procedure: CRITICAL: This is a trust boundary. All inputs are adversarial. 1. Craft malformed 0x13 Cells responses violating format [[hash_0, ...], cells, cell_mask]. 2. Test missing/extra fields, type mismatches, oversized arrays. 3. Verify ACTION-PARSE-CELLS rejects invalid formats at EDGE-PARSE-0x13. 4. Fuzz all fields: hashes (wrong length, invalid encoding), cells (mismatched count), cell_mask (incorrect bits). 5. Test cells count != cell_mask popcount. 6. Verify no crashes, memory corruption, or DoS. 7. Check peer penalties for malformed responses. 8. Test boundary: valid format but semantically invalid data.
executable_checks: Send 0x13 response with missing hash array and verify rejection
Send 0x13 response with missing cells field and verify rejection
Send 0x13 response with missing cell_mask and verify rejection
Send 0x13 response with extra unexpected fields and verify handling
Send hash array with wrong length hashes and verify rejection
Send cells count not matching cell_mask popcount and verify rejection
Send cell_mask with bits set beyond valid range and verify rejection
Fuzz hash encoding (non-hex, truncated) and verify parser robustness
Send oversized arrays (10^6 cells) and verify DoS protection
Send deeply nested structures and verify depth limit enforcement
Verify EDGE-PARSE-0x13 transition only on valid format
Check ACTION-PARSE-CELLS does not crash on any malformed input
Verify memory safety: no buffer overflows or leaks
Confirm peer penalty applied for repeated malformed responses
checklist_item.notes: Traced from PROP-W1-EIP8070-PRECOND-003 covering nodes=[ACTION-PARSE-CELLS], edges=[EDGE-PARSE-0x13]. BOUNDARY GUARD: trust boundary from untrusted P2P input.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for EDGE-FORK-INCOMPATIBLE: Fork compatibility enforcement without premature disconnect

validation_layers: Fork compatibility check with timing constraints

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Identify current fork and next fork boundary. 2. Configure test peer with incompatible nfd value in ENR. 3. Initiate peer discovery and connection. 4. Monitor ACTION-EVALUATE-FORK-COMPATIBILITY execution. 5. Verify that peer is marked as fork-incompatible but connection is NOT terminated before fork boundary. 6. Advance mock clock to fork boundary and verify disconnect occurs. 7. Test with various nfd values: future forks, past forks, malformed values.
executable_checks: Connect to peer with incompatible nfd before fork boundary and verify connection is maintained
Verify peer with incompatible nfd is marked as fork-incompatible in peer metadata
Simulate time advancement to fork boundary and verify disconnect of incompatible peer
Test with nfd pointing to future fork and verify no premature disconnect
Test with nfd pointing to past fork and verify handling
Test with malformed nfd value and verify graceful handling without crash
Verify ACTION-EVALUATE-FORK-COMPATIBILITY reads nfd from peer ENR
checklist_item.notes: Traced from PROP-W2-FULU-DISCOVERY-PRECOND-03 covering edges EDGE-CHECK-FORK, EDGE-FORK-INCOMPATIBLE (boundary guard)

checklist_item.title: Falsify Block Validation Post-condition by Detecting Inconsistent Rejection Behavior
checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Construct test block with known blob_gas_used and parent state. 2. Compute correct excess_blob_gas using EIP-7918 formula. 3. Set block header excess_blob_gas to incorrect value. 4. Submit block through ACTION-COMPARE-HEADER validation. 5. Expected: block reaches STATE-BLOCK-INVALID and is rejected. 6. Test for falsification: block incorrectly accepted, cached, or state partially applied despite rejection. 7. Verify rejection is atomic: no state changes persist if validation fails. 8. Test edge cases: uint64 boundaries, transition between normal/reserve modes.
executable_checks: Create block with header.excess_blob_gas = recalculated_excess + 1, verify block reaches STATE-BLOCK-INVALID
Create block with header.excess_blob_gas = 0 when recalculated value is non-zero, verify rejection
Set header.excess_blob_gas to uint64::MAX when recalculated is small value, verify detection
Submit block with mismatched excess_blob_gas, verify it is not added to fork choice store
Confirm rejected block does not trigger state transition side effects
Test with recalculated_excess = 2^64 - 1 and header.excess = 0, verify overflow handling
Create block where recalculated excess differs due to wrong mode (reserve vs. normal), verify detection
Verify rejection occurs before blob KZG verification (fail fast)
Submit valid block followed by invalid block with same parent, verify invalid block rejected without affecting valid block
Test race condition: submit invalid block during state transition, verify atomic rejection
checklist_item.notes: Traced from PROP-W3-EIP7918-VALIDATION-POST-001 covering ACTION-COMPARE-HEADER, STATE-BLOCK-VALID, STATE-BLOCK-INVALID

checklist_item.title: Falsify Pre-condition: Bypass consistent custody fraction (1/8) by configuring divergent values across nodes
checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Deploy network with 2+ nodes. 2. Configure node A with custody_fraction = 1/8. 3. Attempt to configure node B with different custody_fraction. 4. Submit DAS sampling request. 5. Compare custody assignments between nodes. 6. Verify if hardcoded vs configurable parameter.
executable_checks: Test hardcoded custody fraction: search codebase for configurable custody_fraction parameter, verify none exists
Multi-node test: verify all nodes use identical custody fraction without configuration options
Config file test: attempt to override custody_fraction in config file, verify override rejected or ignored
Runtime modification test: attempt to change custody_fraction via RPC, verify rejection
Spec compliance test: verify custody_fraction = 1/8 matches EIP7594 specification exactly
Fork transition test: verify custody_fraction remains 1/8 across fork boundary
checklist_item.notes: Traced from PROP-W5-EIP7594-DAS-AMB-001 covering custody fraction consistency across network

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for Engine API -> DataColumnSidecar Construction: Cell KZG Proof Validation
checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Identify GetPayloadResponse reception point from Engine API. 2. Trace cell KZG proofs from response to DataColumnSidecar construction. 3. Locate all validation gates between reception and usage. 4. Attempt to bypass validation: send invalid proofs, malformed commitments, mismatched proof counts. 5. Verify if invalid proofs reach sidecar construction.
executable_checks: Send GetPayloadResponse with invalid cell KZG proofs and verify rejection before sidecar construction
Provide mismatched proof count vs cell count and verify validation failure
Submit syntactically valid but cryptographically invalid KZG proofs and verify verification rejects them
Test GetPayloadResponse with proofs referencing non-existent cells and verify boundary check
Simulate compromised EL sending replayed proofs from different payload and verify CL detects mismatch
checklist_item.notes: Traced from PROP-W6-FULU-VAL-SIDECAR-PRECOND-002 covering edge_engine_api_to_sidecar_construction

checklist_item.title: Falsify Polynomial Multiplication Bound: Force len(a) + len(b) - 1 > FIELD_ELEMENTS_PER_EXT_BLOB
checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Identify polynomial recovery code path. 2. Determine inputs to polynomial multiplication. 3. Craft test cases with polynomial lengths such that len(a) + len(b) - 1 exceeds FIELD_ELEMENTS_PER_EXT_BLOB. 4. Trigger recovery with adversarial inputs. 5. Verify if bound check exists and correctly rejects. 6. Test edge cases: maximum length polynomials, off-by-one errors, overflow in length calculation.
executable_checks: Invoke polynomial multiplication with len(a) = FIELD_ELEMENTS_PER_EXT_BLOB and len(b) = 2 and verify rejection
Test recovery with maximum allowed polynomial lengths and verify bound satisfied
Craft missing cell scenario requiring polynomial multiplication that violates bound and verify error handling
Test len(a) + len(b) - 1 = FIELD_ELEMENTS_PER_EXT_BLOB (boundary) and verify acceptance
Test len(a) + len(b) - 1 = FIELD_ELEMENTS_PER_EXT_BLOB + 1 (boundary+1) and verify rejection
checklist_item.notes: Traced from PROP-W7-PCS-RECOVER-INV-001 covering polynomial_multiplication_recovery

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for EDGE-VALIDATOR-RANDAO-TO-LOOKAHEAD: EB lock timing

reachability.notes: Using current EB instead of locked EB enables grinding.

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. At epoch N, note all validators' effective balances. 2. A malicious validator changes their effective balance between epoch N and N+1 (via deposits/withdrawals). 3. Verify that proposer computation for epoch N + MIN_SEED_LOOKAHEAD + 1 uses the EB values from epoch N - MIN_SEED_LOOKAHEAD - 1, NOT the current epoch. 4. Compare proposer lists with historical vs current EB values to detect grinding vulnerability.
executable_checks: Test: modify validator EB after RANDAO reveal, verify proposer schedule unchanged
Test: verify get_beacon_proposer_index uses historical EB snapshot
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W0-EIP7917-PRECOND-002. Boundary edge: EDGE-VALIDATOR-RANDAO-TO-LOOKAHEAD. Anti-grinding security depends on EB lock timing.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for EDGE-PARSE-0x10: RLP nil literal handling in PooledTransactions

reachability.notes: Incorrect handling of RLP nil literals for elided blob payloads could cause type confusion or null pointer dereferences.

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Construct a PooledTransactions (0x10) response containing a type-3 transaction with blob payload fields set to RLP nil literals (0x80). 2. Send via P2P to target node. 3. Verify the node correctly parses nil as 'elided blob payload' (not as empty bytes or as an error). 4. Verify no null pointer dereference or type confusion occurs. 5. Test malformed nil variants (truncated RLP, wrong length prefix).
executable_checks: P2P test: send PooledTransactions with nil blob payload, assert correct parsing
P2P test: send malformed nil literal variants, assert graceful rejection
Cross-client: verify identical behavior for nil literal handling
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W1-EIP8070-PRECOND-004. Boundary edge: EDGE-PARSE-0x10 (EDGE-PEER-CELLS-RESPONSE). Trust boundary for P2P deserialization.

checklist_item.title: Falsify active validator set derivation for proposer computation

reachability.notes: Wrong active validator set would produce incorrect proposer selection.

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. At epoch boundary, compute active validator indices using get_active_validator_indices(state, epoch). 2. Verify the set includes only validators with activation_epoch <= epoch < exit_epoch. 3. Attempt to falsify: check if validators pending activation or pending exit are incorrectly included/excluded. 4. Test edge case: validator whose activation_epoch == target_epoch (should be included).
executable_checks: Test: validator with activation_epoch == epoch, assert included in active set
Test: validator with exit_epoch == epoch, assert excluded from active set
Test: verify proposer computation uses correct epoch's active set
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3-FULU-BEACON-LOOKAHEAD-PRE-002. Pre-condition for proposer lookahead computation.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for BOUNDARY-DAS-COLUMN-SAMPLING: KZG proof verification on P2P column samples

reachability.notes: Malicious P2P peer can send column samples with invalid KZG proofs. If verification is skipped or flawed, node may accept invalid data as available.

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Construct column samples with valid structure but invalid KZG proofs (random bytes for proofs). 2. Send via P2P sampling response. 3. Verify node rejects the column and does NOT count it toward DA confirmation. 4. Test: valid column with one bit-flipped proof byte. 5. Test batch verification: verify a single invalid proof in a batch causes the entire batch to fail.
executable_checks: P2P test: column with random KZG proof bytes, assert rejection
P2P test: column with single-bit-flipped proof, assert rejection
Batch test: batch of 10 columns with 1 invalid, assert batch rejection
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W5-EIP7594-DAS-PRECOND-001. Boundary edge: BOUNDARY-DAS-COLUMN-SAMPLING. CRITICAL: this is the foundation of DAS security.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for sidecar reconstruction: input integrity validation

reachability.notes: A malicious peer could send a crafted sidecar that passes initial checks but causes errors during reconstruction.

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Construct a sidecar with valid headers/signature but corrupted cell data (passes signature check, fails during reconstruction). 2. Send via P2P. 3. Verify the node does not crash during get_data_column_sidecars_from_column_sidecar. 4. Test: sidecar with truncated cell data, extra cells, mismatched column indices. 5. Verify error handling returns a proper error rather than panicking.
executable_checks: P2P test: sidecar with corrupted cell data, assert no crash during reconstruction
P2P test: sidecar with truncated cells, assert graceful rejection
P2P test: sidecar with extra cells, assert rejection
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W6-FULU-VAL-SIDECAR-PRECOND-003. Related ambiguity: AMB-FULU-VALIDATOR-04.

checklist_item.title: Falsify secp256r1 precompile return value format consistency across execution paths
checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Deploy a contract that calls the secp256r1 precompile (0x0b / address TBD per EIP-7951) with a valid signature and checks returndata size == 32 and returndata == 0x0000...0001. 2. Deploy a second contract that calls with invalid signature (wrong r, s, or public key) and checks returndata size == 0. 3. Execute both transactions in a block and verify all client implementations produce identical return values. 4. Edge cases: (a) valid signature but point-at-infinity public key, (b) signature with s > n/2 (malleability), (c) zero-length input, (d) input shorter than 160 bytes. 5. Compare return values across at least two EL client implementations.
executable_checks: Create test transaction calling precompile with valid (r, s, x, y, hash) and assert returndata == bytes32(1)
Create test transaction calling precompile with invalid signature and assert returndata == bytes(0)
Fuzz input lengths from 0 to 200 bytes and verify consistent return across clients
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W0-EIP7951-POSTCOND-001. Post-condition check. Divergent return formats across clients would cause consensus split on any block containing a secp256r1 precompile call. Covers nodes: ACTION-RETURN-SUCCESS, ACTION-RETURN-EMPTY, STATE-PRECOMPILE-COMPLETE.

checklist_item.title: Falsify Fusaka fork identifier correctness after activation
checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Set up a node configured for Fusaka fork at a specific epoch. 2. Advance the node past the fork epoch. 3. Query the node's fork_id (via devp2p Status message or internal API). 4. Verify the fork_id matches the expected value: CRC32 of genesis hash XOR'd with fork block number/timestamp. 5. For mainnet: verify fork_id == 0x5167e2a6 (or whatever the final value is). 6. Cross-check: connect two nodes post-fork and verify they successfully peer (matching fork_ids). 7. Cross-check: connect a pre-fork and post-fork node and verify they reject each other.
executable_checks: Verify fork_id value matches specification after fork activation
Verify two post-fork nodes can peer successfully
Verify pre-fork and post-fork nodes reject peering
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W1-EIP7607-POSTCOND-001. Post-condition falsification. Wrong fork ID would isolate nodes on the network. Covers nodes: ACTION-UPDATE-FORK-ID, STATE-FUSAKA-ACTIVE.

checklist_item.title: Falsify peer custody compliance monitoring and enforcement
checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Connect a malicious peer that advertises custody of specific column groups in its ENR. 2. Request columns from the advertised custody groups. 3. Have the malicious peer fail to respond or return errors. 4. Verify the target node: (a) detects the non-compliance, (b) downscores the peer, (c) eventually disconnects the peer. 5. Verify the node seeks alternative peers for the missing custody groups. 6. Test timing: how long before non-compliant peer is penalized?
executable_checks: Connect non-compliant peer, verify downscore after failed custody requests
Verify eventual disconnection of non-compliant peer
Verify node finds replacement peer for uncovered custody groups
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W2-FULU-DISCOVERY-POSTCOND-01. Post-condition falsification. Failure to enforce custody allows free-riding peers degrading DA capacity.

checklist_item.title: Falsify correct parent block data retrieval for reserve price computation
checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. During block validation, verify the parent block's base_fee_per_gas, excess_blob_gas, and blob_gas_used are read from the correct parent (by parent_hash). 2. Test with a reorg scenario: ensure the correct parent is used after chain reorganization. 3. Verify no caching issues: if the parent was previously read for a different fork, ensure fresh data is used. 4. Edge case: genesis block as parent (no excess_blob_gas). 5. Edge case: first block after Fulu fork (parent is last Electra block).
executable_checks: Verify parent data matches parent_hash, not just previous block number
Test after reorg: verify correct parent data is used
Test first Fulu block reading Electra parent data
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3-EIP7918-RESERVE-PRE-003. Pre-condition falsification. Wrong parent data produces incorrect fee calculations for all subsequent blocks.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for MODEXP modulus length upper bound enforcement
checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Submit a transaction calling MODEXP precompile (address 0x05) with modulus_length = 1025 bytes (one over the 1024 limit). 2. Verify the precompile returns empty (rejects) before performing any computation. 3. Test with modulus_length = 1024 (should succeed). 4. Test with modulus_length = 0 (edge case). 5. Test with modulus_length = 2^32 - 1 (max uint32, if length is 32-bit). 6. Measure execution time for modulus_length = 1024 vs 1025: the 1025 case must return instantly (no computation). 7. Test all three input lengths (base, exponent, modulus) against their respective bounds.
executable_checks: Submit MODEXP with modulus_length = 1025, verify immediate rejection
Submit MODEXP with modulus_length = 1024, verify success
Measure execution time: 1025 must be near-zero (no computation)
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W5-EIP7823-VAL-PRECOND-003. Boundary property. Trust boundary: attacker-controlled calldata via on-chain transaction. Unbounded modulus causes excessive computation potentially crashing all clients.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for worst-case block size within 10 MiB gossip limit at 60M gas
checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Construct an adversarial block maximizing serialized size within 60M gas: (a) maximum calldata (16 gas/byte non-zero, 4 gas/byte zero), (b) maximum contract creation initcode, (c) maximum LOG operations with data, (d) maximum RETURN data. 2. Measure the RLP-serialized size of the block. 3. Verify it is strictly < 10 MiB. 4. Try novel constructions: combine multiple size-maximizing strategies. 5. Account for: block header, transaction list overhead, uncle/ommers (should be empty post-merge). 6. If a construction exceeds 10 MiB, this is a critical finding: CL would reject valid EL blocks.
executable_checks: Construct max-calldata block at 60M gas, measure serialized size
Construct max-LOG-data block at 60M gas, measure serialized size
Verify all constructions produce size < 10 MiB
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W7-EIP7935-SECBND-INV-001. Boundary property. Related assumption: ASSUM-EIP7935-03. If violated, attacker produces valid EL blocks that CL rejects, causing consensus-execution layer split affecting all nodes. Requires PoC: construct a block within 60M gas whose serialized size >= 10 MiB.

checklist_item.title: Falsify validation completeness: attempt block acceptance with each individual assertion failing
checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. For each validation check in on_block (parent known, slot ordering, finalized slot, finalized ancestry, data availability): craft a block that fails ONLY that specific check while passing all others. 2. Send each crafted block via P2P gossip. 3. Verify each block is rejected and does NOT enter the block store. 4. Specifically test: (a) unknown parent root, (b) slot <= parent slot, (c) slot <= finalized slot, (d) not descendant of finalized block, (e) missing data columns. 5. Verify no partial state is written on rejection.
executable_checks: Assert block with unknown parent_root is rejected
Assert block with slot <= parent.slot is rejected
Assert block failing finalized ancestry check is rejected
Assert block with missing data availability is rejected
Assert store.blocks does not contain any rejected block's root
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W0-FULUFC-INV-002. Invariant type - testing each rejection path independently. Critical: if any single assertion can be bypassed, invalid blocks corrupt fork choice for all nodes. Covers edges EDGE-PARENT-FAIL, EDGE-SLOT-FAIL, EDGE-FINALIZED-SLOT-FAIL, EDGE-ANCESTRY-FAIL, EDGE-DATA-FAIL.

checklist_item.title: Falsify multi-provider requirement: attempt cell requests with single provider
checklist_item.bug_class: Single Point of Failure / Selective Withholding
checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Set up a sampler node. 2. Have only 1 peer announce as a provider for a specific blob. 3. Verify the sampler waits and does NOT request cells until at least 2 distinct providers are observed. 4. Add a second provider. Verify the sampler proceeds. 5. Test edge case: 2 announcements from same peer (should count as 1 provider). 6. Verify timeout behavior if second provider never appears.
executable_checks: Assert sampler blocks cell requests until >= 2 distinct providers observed
Assert duplicate announcements from same peer count as 1 provider
Assert sampler proceeds once 2 distinct providers announce
Assert timeout mechanism exists for insufficient providers
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W1-EIP8070-PRECOND-005. Pre-condition type. Related assumption ASSUM-EIP8070-03. Single-provider requests enable undetectable selective withholding.

checklist_item.title: Verify anti-equivocation cache is updated for locally-imported columns (not just gossip)
checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Have a proposer create a block with blobs. 2. Import the data columns via the local EL path (engine_getBlobsV2). 3. Check if the anti-equivocation cache now contains (slot, proposer_index, column_index) for each imported column. 4. Attempt to import a DIFFERENT set of columns for the same (slot, proposer_index) via gossip. 5. Verify the second import is detected as equivocation and REJECTED. 6. If the cache was NOT updated in step 3, the conflicting columns would be accepted, causing a consensus split.
executable_checks: Assert cache contains entries after local EL import
Assert conflicting columns for same (slot, proposer) detected as equivocation
Assert equivocation detection works identically for gossip and local paths
Assert cache entries match (slot, proposer_index, column_index) tuple format
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W2-FULU-BLOB-PUB-POSTCOND-01. Post-condition type. Related assumption ASSUM-FULU-P2P-05. Critical: equivocation detection must work for all import paths, not just gossip. Cache miss on local path enables consensus splits.

checklist_item.title: Falsify BPO parameter irreversibility: test chain reorg across fork boundary
checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Process blocks past a BPO fork activation point. Verify blob parameters are post-BPO values. 2. Trigger a chain reorg that goes back before the BPO activation. 3. Re-process the canonical chain. 4. Verify blob parameters are STILL post-BPO after reorg resolution (if the reorg target is after BPO). 5. If the reorg reverts to before BPO, verify parameters correctly revert only temporarily and re-activate when the chain catches up to BPO timestamp. 6. Verify no state corruption from the reorg.
executable_checks: Assert blob parameters remain post-BPO after reorg that stays after activation
Assert parameters correctly transition during reorg across boundary
Assert no state corruption from fork boundary reorg
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W4-EIP7892-INV-002. Invariant type. Parameter reversion after fork activation would cause consensus divergence on reorgs across the BPO fork boundary.

checklist_item.title: Verify gas cap enforcement in block validation rejects blocks with over-cap transactions
checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Craft a valid-looking block where one transaction has gasLimit = 2^24 + 1. 2. Send via P2P. Verify entire block is rejected. 3. Test: what if the block was produced by a malicious validator who bypassed their own mempool checks? Verify receiving nodes still reject. 4. Verify the check is in the block validation path, not just mempool.
executable_checks: Assert block with over-cap transaction is rejected during block validation
Assert validation is independent of mempool (works for received blocks)
Assert rejection reason is clearly gas limit cap violation
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W5-EIP7825-TX-POSTCOND-001. Supporting node check for block-level validation. Trust model edge BOUNDARY-BLOCK-GAS-LIMIT-P2P requires per-transaction gasLimit check in received blocks.

checklist_item.title: Verify compute_cells_and_kzg_proofs produces exactly CELLS_PER_EXT_BLOB cells and proofs
checklist_item.bug_class: Cell Count Mismatch / PeerDAS Distribution Failure
checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Call compute_cells_and_kzg_proofs with a valid blob. 2. Count the returned cells. Assert count == CELLS_PER_EXT_BLOB. 3. Count the returned proofs. Assert count == CELLS_PER_EXT_BLOB. 4. Test with various blob contents: all zeros, all ones, random, maximum field elements. 5. Verify cell indices are [0, CELLS_PER_EXT_BLOB). 6. Test with a blob of all zero bytes (valid) and verify output shape.
executable_checks: Assert len(cells) == CELLS_PER_EXT_BLOB
Assert len(proofs) == CELLS_PER_EXT_BLOB
Assert each cell has correct byte length (BYTES_PER_CELL)
Assert each proof has correct byte length (BYTES_PER_PROOF)
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W7-PCS-ENCODE-POSTCOND-002. Post-condition type. Wrong cell count would cause all peers requesting specific cell indices to fail verification, potentially affecting >33% of the network.

checklist_item.title: Falsify invariant: The retrieve_column_sidecars function must return exactly the set of column sidecars needed for the node's DAS sampling

reachability.notes: Attacker can selectively withhold sidecars from targeted nodes.

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Identify the invariant at ACTION-RETRIEVE-COLUMN-SIDECARS: The retrieve_column_sidecars function must return exactly the set of column sidecars needed for the node's DAS sampling requirements. Implementation behavior is context-dependent but must be consistent. 2. Design state transitions that could violate this invariant. 3. Test boundary conditions (min/max values, empty inputs, overflow). 4. Execute transitions and verify the invariant holds after each step. 5. If externally reachable, craft on-chain transactions or P2P messages to trigger violations. 6. Cross-client comparison to detect implementation divergence.
executable_checks: Construct state transition sequence targeting ACTION-RETRIEVE-COLUMN-SIDECARS
Test with boundary values (0, max, overflow)
Verify invariant holds across all transitions
Cross-client comparison if consensus-relevant
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W0-FULUFC-INV-003. Mindset: Formal Verification Engineer. Type: Invariant. Primary: ACTION-RETRIEVE-COLUMN-SIDECARS. Nodes: ['ACTION-RETRIEVE-COLUMN-SIDECARS', 'STATE-SIDECARS-RETRIEVED']. Edges: ['EDGE-INITIATE-RETRIEVAL', 'EDGE-RETRIEVAL-SUCCESS', 'EDGE-RETRIEVAL-FAIL'].

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for EDGE-CHECK-KZG: validate invariant at entry point

reachability.notes: This is a cryptographic assumption. If broken, the entire DAS scheme fails.

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Identify the trust boundary at EDGE-CHECK-KZG where untrusted data enters via P2P. 2. Review input validation: An attacker must not be able to forge valid KZG proofs for cells without possessing the actual blob data corresponding to the commitments. 3. Craft malicious input that violates the stated precondition. 4. Send via P2P and observe whether the node accepts, rejects, or crashes. 5. Compare behavior across at least 2 client implementations for consensus divergence.
executable_checks: Craft malformed input targeting EDGE-CHECK-KZG boundary
Send via P2P/transaction to a test node
Verify rejection with correct error code
Cross-client comparison of acceptance/rejection behavior
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W1-DASCORE-INV-006. Mindset: Boundary Guard. Property: An attacker must not be able to forge valid KZG proofs for cells without possessing the actual blob data corresponding to the commitments.. Graph: nodes=['ACTION-VERIFY-CELL-KZG-PROOFS'], edges=['EDGE-CHECK-KZG'].

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for EDGE-RECEIVE-UPDATE: validate pre-condition at entry point

reachability.notes: Attacker can spam BlockRangeUpdate messages to flood peer databases

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Identify the trust boundary at EDGE-RECEIVE-UPDATE where untrusted data enters via P2P BlockRangeUpdate messages. 2. Review input validation: Before accepting a BlockRangeUpdate from a peer, the node SHOULD enforce rate limiting to maximum once per epoch (32 blocks) per peer as recommended by AMB-EIP7642-01. Peers exceeding this rate MAY be downscored but MUST NOT be disconnected solely for rate limit violations. 3. Craft malicious input that violates the stated precondition. 4. Send via P2P BlockRangeUpdate messages and observe whether the node accepts, rejects, or crashes. 5. Compare behavior across at least 2 client implementations for consensus divergence.
executable_checks: Craft malformed input targeting EDGE-RECEIVE-UPDATE boundary
Send via P2P/transaction to a test node
Verify rejection with correct error code
Cross-client comparison of acceptance/rejection behavior
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W2-EIP7642-HISTORY-PRECOND-02. Mindset: Boundary Guard. Property: Before accepting a BlockRangeUpdate from a peer, the node SHOULD enforce rate limiting to maximum once per epoch (32 blocks) per peer as recommended by AMB-EIP7642-01. Peers exceeding this rate MAY be. Graph: nodes=['ACTION-RECEIVE-BLOCK-RANGE-UPDATE', 'STATE-PEER-HISTORY-KNOWN'], edges=['EDGE-RECEIVE-UPDATE', 'EDGE-UPDATE-PEER-KNOWLEDGE'].

checklist_item.title: Falsify invariant: During the fork transition from pre-Fulu (STATE-PRE-FULU-EPOCH) to post-Fulu (STATE-FULU-EPOCH-REACHED and beyond), at l

reachability.notes: Q4 scaffolding: continuous DA availability during fork

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Identify the invariant at STATE-TRANSITION-PERIOD: During the fork transition from pre-Fulu (STATE-PRE-FULU-EPOCH) to post-Fulu (STATE-FULU-EPOCH-REACHED and beyond), at least one protocol (blob sidecars OR data columns) MUST be serving data availability for every slot in the active serving window. This is the Q4 scaffolding invariant ensuring continuous DA during transition. 2. Design state transitions that could violate this invariant. 3. Test boundary conditions (min/max values, empty inputs, overflow). 4. Execute transitions and verify the invariant holds after each step. 5. If externally reachable, craft on-chain transactions or P2P messages to trigger violations. 6. Cross-client comparison to detect implementation divergence.
executable_checks: Construct state transition sequence targeting STATE-TRANSITION-PERIOD

reachability.notes: Standard EVM gas metering behavior, but must be consistent across clients with new pricing.

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Execute the operation at STATE-OUT-OF-GAS. 2. Verify the postcondition: After gas charging, if the caller's remaining gas is less than the computed ModExp gas cost, the call must revert with out-of-gas and consume all provided gas. 3. Check for unexpected side effects or state changes. 4. Test with edge-case inputs that stress the postcondition. 5. Cross-client comparison of output states.

reachability.notes: Attacker who controls RANDAO reveal at the epoch before fork could bias proposer selection for the initial Fulu epoch.

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Identify the invariant at ACTION-INITIALIZE-PROPOSER-LOOKAHEAD: An attacker MUST NOT be able to manipulate the randomness seed used by initialize_proposer_lookahead to bias proposer selection at the fork boundary. 2. Design state transitions that could violate this invariant. 3. Test boundary conditions (min/max values, empty inputs, overflow). 4. Execute transitions and verify the invariant holds after each step. 5. If externally reachable, craft on-chain transactions or P2P messages to trigger violations. 6. Cross-client comparison to detect implementation divergence.

checklist_item.title: Verify post-boundary handling at STATE-TX-SUBMITTED

reachability.notes: Attacker can submit transactions via P2P with arbitrarily high gas limits.

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. After data crosses boundary at EDGE-TX-SUBMIT, verify internal handling at STATE-TX-SUBMITTED. 2. Check that validated data is correctly processed: A transaction submitted to the mempool MUST have its gas limit validated against the block gas limit (60M post-Fusaka) before acceptance. 3. Verify no TOCTOU between boundary validation and internal use. 4. Confirm state transitions are correct post-validation.
